title: meta/batch_execute: Tool Chaining, Naming, and Failure Handling
summary: Canonical contract for meta/batch_execute — the Mosaic tool that chains N sub-calls into one MCP round-trip. Covers the input shape (Calls[] of {ToolName, Arguments}), the lowercase/slash naming rule (terrain/height, not terrain_height or Terrain.Height), sequential non-atomic execution, per-call result inspection, StopOnError, Undo collapsing, and the failure modes LLM clients hit when guessing the format. Read before chaining more than three mutation calls.

llmGuidance.when_making_more_than_three_tool_calls_i: Wrap them in meta/batch_execute. Each sub-call is {ToolName: 'category/action', Arguments: {...}}. One Undo group, one response.
llmGuidance.when_writing_a_sub_call_tool_name: Use the exact MosaicTool attribute string: lowercase, slash-separated, kebab-case after the slash. Never underscore, dot, PascalCase, or client prefixes (mcp_mosaic_bridge_*).
llmGuidance.when_a_sub_call_needs_output_from_an_ear: Batch is feed-forward — no placeholder substitution. Split into two batches so the first result is readable before composing the second, or reference by name/path instead of GUID.
llmGuidance.when_chaining_across_async_unity_work: Do NOT batch across a compile, asset-import, or bake. Those are deferred; the next sub-call sees stale state. Split at the async edge and run editor/refresh between batches.
llmGuidance.when_the_batch_response_contains_any_suc: Default is keep-running: sub-calls after the failure still ran. Inspect every Results[].Success and remediate per-entry. Set StopOnError=true only when later sub-calls would corrupt state.
llmGuidance.when_a_planned_batch_exceeds_roughly_fif: Split it. Oversize MCP responses truncate at the transport. Fifty is conservative — 100 tiny calls are fine, 50 heavy scene-returns are not.
llmGuidance.when_the_tool_name_itself_is_uncertain: Never invent. Call meta/list_tools and copy the registered name verbatim. Registry lookup is exact-match; any drift returns 'Tool not found'.

inputShape.Calls: Required. Non-empty array of BatchCall: {ToolName, Arguments}.
inputShape.StopOnError: Optional bool, default false. When true, first failure skips all remaining entries.

batchCallShape.ToolName: Required. Exact MosaicTool attribute name — lowercase category/action.
batchCallShape.Arguments: Optional JSON object matching the target's param schema. Omit for parameterless tools.

outputShape.SkippedCount: Sub-calls not executed due to StopOnError.
outputShape.Results: Array of BatchCallResult in input order: {ToolName, Success, Data, Error, DurationMs, Skipped}.

namingContract.rule: ToolName must equal the string passed to [MosaicTool("...")] on the handler method.

incorrectExamples.reason: Underscore normalization — most LLM tool layers do this automatically; undo it before emitting.
Dot + PascalCase — looks like a .NET API, not a MosaicTool name.
PascalCase action. Actions are kebab-case: terrain/height with Action='set' in Arguments.
Client-side MCP prefix. The registry sees only the bare MosaicTool name; prefixes are your client's concern, not the server's.
Leading slash — the registry key has no leading slash.
The batch tool itself is meta/batch_execute. Dropping the meta/ prefix inside a sub-call fails lookup.

executionSemantics.atomicity: NOT atomic. A mid-batch failure does not roll back earlier mutations. StopOnError halts forward progress, but prior changes still persist.
executionSemantics.undoGrouping: All sub-calls collapse into ONE Editor Undo entry labeled 'Mosaic: Batch Execute'. One Ctrl+Z reverts the whole batch.
executionSemantics.statefulness: Sub-call N sees the scene produced by sub-calls 1..N-1. No runtime templating of earlier outputs into later inputs — the LLM must supply any cross-call identifiers literally at batch-compose time.
executionSemantics.argumentBinding: Each Arguments object is bound via ParameterValidator.Bind against the target tool's param type. Same rules as a direct call: required fields present, types match, case-sensitive keys.
executionSemantics.errorIsolation: Sub-call exceptions are caught — the batch itself never throws. Each failure becomes Success:false with a trimmed Error message.

whenBatchWins: Bulk writes to the same asset (many terrain or mesh edits). One Undo entry, N-1 saved round-trips.
Scene bootstrap: create → add component → set property, repeated across a small graph.
Independent spawns: 10 prefab variants with different transforms.
Prewritten recipes that need no intermediate inspection.
whenBatchLoses: Conditional logic — LLM must see sub-call N's result before composing N+1.
Crossing async boundaries: AssetDatabase.Refresh, shader compile, light bake, script reload.
Oversize responses (hundreds of scene snapshots). Split at ~50 sub-calls.

decisionRules.scenario: About to issue N > 3 tool calls with no branching between them
decisionRules.rule: Collapse into one meta/batch_execute. Wrapper cost is trivial; protocol savings scale linearly.
decisionRules.evidence: Gemini terrain session 2026-04-21 § G7 — 40+ serial terrain/height calls hit the API rate limit mid-scene.

decisionRules.scenario: Unsure of a sub-call's tool name format
decisionRules.rule: Query meta/list_tools once per session and copy names verbatim. No case, separator, or prefix transforms.
decisionRules.evidence: Gemini terrain session § G5 — 36/36 sub-calls failed on client-side prefixing.

decisionRules.scenario: Sub-call 2 needs an ID produced by sub-call 1
decisionRules.rule: Split into two batches, or reference by name/path instead of GUID.

decisionRules.scenario: Batch crosses a compile, asset import, or bake
decisionRules.rule: Split at the async boundary. Run editor/refresh between batches.
decisionRules.evidence: Unity async ops complete after the current tool returns — subsequent sub-calls see stale state.

decisionRules.scenario: Batch exceeds ~50 sub-calls or returns large payloads
decisionRules.rule: Split. Adjust downward for tools that return scene snapshots or hierarchies.
decisionRules.evidence: MCP transport frame size is finite.

decisionRules.scenario: A mid-batch failure would leave the scene unrepairable
decisionRules.rule: Set StopOnError=true. Prior mutations still persist — plan a compensating batch if rollback is required.
decisionRules.evidence: BatchExecuteParams.StopOnError aborts forward progress only; no automatic rollback.

commonPitfalls.symptom: All sub-calls return 'Tool not found' though the tool works when called directly.
commonPitfalls.cause: Client-side tool-name transform (underscore, prefix, case) leaking into Calls[].ToolName. Registry lookup is exact-match.
commonPitfalls.fix: Emit the MosaicTool attribute name verbatim.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Argument binding fails with 'Required field missing'.
commonPitfalls.cause: Case-mismatch on a parameter key. Binding is case-sensitive.
commonPitfalls.fix: Match the exact C# property name from the tool's Params class.

commonPitfalls.cause: Arguments was a scalar or array, not a JSON object.
commonPitfalls.fix: Always wrap: {"key": value}. Even single-arg tools expect an object.

commonPitfalls.cause: StopOnError=true aborted the batch after an earlier failure; all subsequent entries were Skipped:true.
commonPitfalls.fix: Fix the earlier call, or set StopOnError=false to observe which calls would have succeeded.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Scene shows partial changes after a failed batch.
commonPitfalls.cause: Caller assumed batch was atomic. It isn't — prior mutations persist.
commonPitfalls.fix: Inspect every Results[].Success. For all-or-nothing work, write a custom macro tool.
Split at ~50 sub-calls; lower for chatty tools.

workedExamples.title: Spawn a 10-cube grid
workedExamples.note: Truncated to 2 for brevity. Ten fits comfortably under the 50-call ceiling; one Undo entry covers the whole grid.

workedExamples.title: ANTI-PATTERN — 500 per-point terrain/height stamps
workedExamples.antiPattern: One terrain/height sub-call per heightmap sample, 500 entries in one batch.
workedExamples.whyWrong: Every stamp re-reads and re-writes overlapping neighborhoods. The right shape is a single multi-point call (terrain/stroke or terrain/stamp — see kb terrain/height-tool-decision-tree).
workedExamples.correctApproach: Use the highest-level terrain primitive available. Fall back to batched terrain/height only when no shape-level tool exists, and cap per-batch at ~50.

workedExamples.title: Create material + set 3 properties + assign
workedExamples.note: StopOnError=true fits here — downstream calls reference RedMetal by name. Referring by name instead of GUID dodges the output-dependency problem entirely.

versionDeltas.change: Undo.SetCurrentGroupName + Undo.CollapseUndoOperations behave identically across 2021.3 → 6000.4. Batch Undo collapsing relies on stable Editor APIs.

sources.title: Mosaic Bridge — BatchExecuteTool.cs (source of truth for semantics)

authorshipNotes: Content authored by Mosaic team. The tool contract (input shape, output shape, execution semantics, Undo grouping, StopOnError behavior) is derived verbatim from the Mosaic Bridge source at packages/com.mosaic.bridge/Editor/Tools/Meta/BatchExecuteTool.cs and is factual (not copyrightable). Failure-mode catalog, decision rules, and worked examples are original Mosaic editorial content, informed by the 2026-04-21 Gemini terrain session (gap-analysis/terrain.md § G5) where 36/36 batched sub-calls failed due to client-side tool-name prefixing. MCP specification is cited as an external reference and NOT reproduced here.
